ogTitle: Towaninny South vs Towong: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Towaninny South and Towong.
heading: Towaninny South vs Towong — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Towaninny South, Victoria and Towong, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Towong has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Towaninny South (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Towaninny South or Towong safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Towaninny South has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Towong scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Towaninny South scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Towong scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Towaninny South or Towong?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Towong has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
